libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not add Hibernate, EclipseLink, H2, or a database container just to compile or exercise the API. Keep coverage focused on the Jakarta Persistence API surface using in-test stubs/fakes. For 4.0.0-M5, Query and TypedQuery include the new setParameters(Object...) method, so any local Query/TypedQuery test doubles must implement it.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8596 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.persistence:jakarta.persistence-api:4.0.0-M5; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: A generated integration-style test that calls Persistence.createEntityManagerFactory without installing a fake PersistenceProviderResolver or adding a real provider will fail because the API artifact does not include an implementation.
Adding a real provider/database would shift coverage toward the provider rather than the jakarta.persistence-api artifact and is not required for the reported javac failure.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.persistence-api is an API-only artifact; its only Maven dependency is the normal transitive compile dependency on jakarta.annotation-api, and meaningful API coverage can be reached with annotations, SPI/resolver stubs, and in-test EntityManager/Query implementations without a real JPA provider, database, Docker image, or system properties.
